title: Talking Heads

{{CSS|User:PortulTrademark/MarbleCoasts.css}} __NOTOC__ {{User:PortulTrademark/MarbleCoastsCSS}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Talking Heads}} {{CSS|content=body { filter: grayscale(100%); } }} __NOTOC__ =
'''"Talking Heads" by Helena Keina'''
=
Talking Heads are a collection of 5 salt statues installed across various Backrooms levels in 1998. The artist, Helena "Jill" KeinaKnown online as "JeffTheJiller" confirmed them each as her own, and has stated a lack of interest in producing more of Object 42. When wanderers step into a 5 meter radius of Object 42, they activate and start to speak. The topics the statues are able to speak on are perfectly emblamatic of the human it was based on, and only acquire new information through interacting. None of them have shown distaste with their situation, and have jokingly adivsed wanderers who spoke to them to also turn into a Talking Head. Object 42 is unaffected by rain and any other environmental damage, retaining perfect shape and quality.
How the statues were placed and given the ability to speak remain unanswered. But when Helena was asked for an explanation on the artwork, she provided an artist statement. This artist statement has been inscribed below, and all other attempts to reach out to her about the work have failed.
{{Collapse|num=1|final=|body= |initial= ===
"What inspired you to make this?"
=== ---- [[File:TalkingHead3.png|thumb|350px|center|
Gincarlo Phillip, whose work helped Judah Casper create the Numbering System. Gincarlo Phillip statue installed in [[Level 145]].
]] The idea for Talking Heads started when I was looking through the Ncipetherazi Library on Level -406. I was doing some art study on facial anatomy and how each society chose to capture ones likeness, so a library with a lot of old books made sense. But, as I was roaming through the shelves of books, I wandered into the history section. One of the books that I found was one about the history of Casper-Bray, and that it dated all the way back to a group called "Protection And Understanding of Liminality". I found it extremely fascinating, and just kept digging. Oddly, when I moved to the science sections and looked into the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]], there was no information about the history of it. A whole chunk of progress was purposefully voided. That's what I first thought at least, but the more books I read, it became more obvious that people simply didn't know about the past. Obviously, I didn't either, but it felt wrong. Tezo's, much like Ncipetheraz and PAUL,Shorthand for Protection And Understanding of Liminality is a very old group. Founded back in the 1200s I think. Nevertheless, it made no sense to me at the time. That's where I started. I created Talking Heads to represent history that is barely looked over. When history is in your face, you can't do much to avoid it, yea?
Click to continue
 |body={{Collapse|num=2|final= |initial= ===
"How did you make this?"
=== ---- [[File:TalkingHead4.png|thumb|250px|center|
Ethel Tezo, founder of Tezo's Art Supplies. Statue installed in [[Level 201]].
]] The art started out with me wanting to use marble. But marble is really hard to come by in the Backrooms, and I didn't want to break the budget of Tezo's Art Supplies just for this piece. I remember speaking to one of our collaborators about this idea, who now works with MEG. She suggested I go to [[Level 620]] and use some of their anomalous salt. It was far cheaper than the marble and I thought it would be an easy project. Salt is rather malleable and all. Damn was I wrong. The salt in the level is anomalous because it takes so long to dissolve. You can't easily form the compound just by controlling the pour of water. But it's also extremely brittle, meaning if i just use a chisel or some other stronger tool, the whole thing would fall apart. It was an odd balance that made me sometimes question my wish to even continue on it. I think the only reason I kept going was the research side of it. For a lot of the project, I would go to [[Level 906]] or Level -406 to read about the various people that aren't as well recognized in Backrooms history. I was a miner, digging through rocks and rubble to find shiny metals or gems. Except those gemstones were small pieces of information about how they looked or how they spoke. Each kernel was apart of a greater puzzle that plagued every second of the project. I have to thank Ohfzed and Yi for coming over to the art station to give me support and food when I'd forget. But I'd absolutely do it again, just not the same piece.
Click to continue
 |body={{Collapse|num=3|final= |initial= ===
"What do you expect from your audience?"
=== ---- [[File:TalkingHead2.png|thumb|250px|center|
Democritus, founder of The Eckva Association. Statue installed in [[Level 223]].
]] I think assuming anything from your audience―especially in the society that the Backrooms―would be a bad idea. Survival is the greatest focus of most who travel through the Backrooms, so the thought of observing the [[Level 725|vanity]] is so far beyond most. I don't blame them, I may be born and raised in this [[Republic of Aurielle|weird hellscape]], but I've had to deal with my share of struggles, especially when it comes to food. It's why Tezo's Art Supplies was moved to [[Level 114]], even if it doesn't tend to have food. I hope that some will have the space to listen to them speak, but with such a focus on the present, it's hard to stay idyllic. History does have a place in here. My studies have shown me patterns and traits that we fall for again and again, ones that we could solve so many years ago if people just looked into history. The information has made me a better friend, a better leader, and a better supporter. We don't need to keep history away from those fighting to survive, there's a way to have both. It might be why I align with parties like [[Architect Industries]]... the goal of safety and survival are pretty important. I don't think we'll get there though, until we look into the past. There may be some surprise though, but really it shouldn't take long for those more familiar to Backrooms society to realize it's a Tezo's installation. Nor would there be much of a way to conceal that part. Maybe we can talk someone into joining us, or maybe find some others who want to continue the project, but until then, I'm happy with what I've made.
Click to continue
 |body={{Collapse|num=4|final= |initial= ===
"Why base it on Backrooms history rather than a specific group?"
=== ---- [[File:TalkingHead1.png|thumb|250px|center|
Evliya Alemşah, a pseudofictonal figure associated with the revolution against Eckva. Statue found in [[Level 571]].
]] Weirdly enough, I really wanted to. I wanted to make all my statues about Aurielle and that shit. My problem was that I couldn't find enough about them. I know they're a new group and all, but so much information has been just lacking. I'm really not one for all the war history and such conflicts, so when I tried to look into the other parts of Aurielle, I'd come back with very little. The documentation I needed to create these statues were few and far between. There was a lot of guesswork that had to go into each one that just felt wrong. There was a piece of the past that I didn't have access to, a puzzle lacking pieces. You can only go so far before you have to draw in the rest. In this case, mold. Regardless, the struggle to make those likenesses could have been so much worse if I kept it to one group. The same reason why I don't think I'll ever make more, the struggle of creating them and bringing them to life felt impossible every step of the process.
Click to continue
 |body={{Collapse|num=5|final= |initial= ===
"What do you want to communicate with this?"
=== ---- [[File:TalkingHead5.png|thumb|250px|center|
Deng Liufan, Backrooms wanderer whole helped Ncipetheraz through the Great Pause. Statue installed in [[Level 231]].
]] The Backrooms is a place of mystical properties and component, its something even the oldest groups know very little about. There is no truth in the idea that history will give us all the answers. That's not my argument to make. I made these to open up the possibility to learn, to make more of what exists. History won't give us the solution to our problems, but it'll prevent the same mistakes from happening again. At least, that's what I think of it all. I'm no genius, just a dumbass with an opinion, yea? [[File:TezosLogo.png|frameless|center|400px]]
